seo.title: Surat Thani: Complete Travel Guide 2026 — Islands, Khao Sok & Gulf Coast Gateway
seo.metaDescription: Plan your Surat Thani trip with our complete 2026 guide. Ferry connections to Koh Samui, Koh Phangan & Koh Tao, Khao Sok National Park, Wat Phra Borommathat Chaiya, and the best night markets in southern Thailand.

overview: Surat Thani is southern Thailand's most important transit hub — the gateway to Koh Samui, Koh Phangan, and Koh Tao — yet it rewards travelers who pause long enough to explore the city itself. Sitting at the mouth of the Tapi River (southern Thailand's longest at 230 km) where it meets the Gulf of Thailand, the city known locally as Ban Don ('city of virtuous people' by royal decree of Rama VI in 1915) combines an authentic working-Thai atmosphere with genuinely impressive day-trip options. Within two hours of the city center you can explore Khao Sok National Park — home to one of the world's oldest rainforests and the stunning Cheow Lan Lake with its floating bungalows — or visit Wat Phra Borommathat Chaiya, a 1,200-year-old Srivijaya-era temple enshrining Buddha relics. The city itself offers excellent street food at the Talad Kaset and Saturday Night Market, a scenic Tapi River waterfront, and the annual Chak Phra festival in October when decorated boats parade along the river. Surat Thani is emphatically not just a stopover.
highlights: Donsak Ferry Terminal — the main gateway to Koh Samui (2 hrs), Koh Phangan (3.5 hrs), and Koh Tao (6 hrs), with multiple daily departures by Raja Ferry, Seatran, and Lomprayah
Khao Sok National Park — 80 km northwest, home to one of Earth's oldest rainforests, jungle trekking, cave tours, and overnight floating bungalows on Cheow Lan Lake
Wat Phra Borommathat Chaiya — a 1,200-year-old Srivijaya-era royal temple 54 km north with a 24-meter prasad-style chedi enshrining Buddha relics, flanked by 108 ancient Buddha images
Saturday Night Market — Surat Thani's massive weekly street market with clothes, food, and local goods at genuine local prices, far less tourist-inflated than markets in resort towns
Tapi River waterfront (Tapee Pier) — evening riverside stalls, longtail boat rides, fresh oysters, and a laidback atmosphere locals love
Chak Phra Festival (October) — one of southern Thailand's most spectacular Buddhist events, with elaborate illuminated boats parading along the Tapi River at the end of Buddhist Lent
Ban Don Floating Market — weekend morning market on the water at Bang Bai Mai canal (Sundays 9 AM-3 PM), 15 minutes from the city center
Pra Cha Rat Floating Market — lively weekend canal market near Wat Bang Mai Bai temple, ideal for local snacks and produce
Oyster farms and seafood scene — the Surat Thani coast and estuary produce excellent oysters; local restaurants along the Tapi River serve them fresh for 50-100 THB per plate
Gateway to Khao Lak — 1.5 hours south, a popular Andaman coast beach resort town easily combined with a Surat Thani visit

bestTimeToVisit.peak: Dec-Apr: Gulf of Thailand dry season brings clear skies, calm seas, and the best conditions for ferry crossings to the islands. January and February are the coolest months (25-30C) and ideal for both city exploration and Khao Sok day trips. Hotel prices rise 20-40% during peak Christmas/New Year weeks.
bestTimeToVisit.shoulder: Nov: The tail end of the rainy season often gives way to beautiful clear days and the sea calms quickly. Much cheaper than December despite similar conditions. The Chak Phra festival falls in late October making October-November culturally interesting.
bestTimeToVisit.lowSeason: May-Oct: Southwest monsoon brings regular heavy rain — afternoon deluges are common June-September. Ferry services to the islands continue year-round but can be disrupted in rough weather. Khao Sok and Wat Phra Borommathat Chaiya are year-round attractions. Hotel prices drop 30-40%.
bestTimeToVisit.recommendation: Visit December to April for the best island connections and beach weather on Koh Samui and Koh Phangan. For Khao Sok specifically, October-November is excellent — the lake levels are high after the rains and wildlife is active. The Chak Phra Festival in October is a compelling reason to time your visit to Surat Thani city itself.

topAttractions.name: Khao Sok National Park
topAttractions.description: One of the world's most biodiverse rainforests, believed to be older than the Amazon at 160 million years. The park encompasses 739 sq km of jungle, rivers, caves, and the stunning Cheow Lan Lake — a vast man-made reservoir ringed by 960-meter limestone karsts. Wildlife includes Malayan tapirs, sun bears, sambar deer, leopard cats, and over 300 bird species. Day trips from Surat Thani are popular (80 km, 1.5 hrs) but an overnight stay on floating bungalows on Cheow Lan Lake is the definitive experience.
topAttractions.location: 80 km northwest of Surat Thani city, Khao Sok village
topAttractions.entranceFee: 200 THB for foreigners (~$6 USD), 40 THB for Thais
tips: Book floating bungalows on Cheow Lan Lake well in advance — only a handful of operators have permits and they fill up
Day trips from Surat Thani typically cost 1,500-2,500 THB including transport, guide, and lunch
Early morning is best for wildlife spotting — animals are most active at dawn before the heat builds

topAttractions.name: Wat Phra Borommathat Chaiya
topAttractions.description: One of southern Thailand's most historically significant temples, dating back over 1,200 years to the Srivijaya empire. The centerpiece is a 24-meter prasad-style chedi (resembling a castle, unique in Thailand) on a cruciform base with four porticoes, enshrining relics of the Buddha. The cloister surrounding the stupa contains 108 ancient Buddha images. The temple is in Chaiya district, 54 km north of Surat Thani town, and is considered one of the three sacred sites most revered in southern Thailand.
topAttractions.location: Chaiya district, 54 km north of Surat Thani town
tips: Combine with a visit to the Chaiya National Museum nearby, which holds Srivijaya-era artifacts
Dress conservatively — cover shoulders and knees as this is an active royal temple
The large car park makes it easy to visit by rental car or scooter from Surat Thani

topAttractions.name: Donsak Ferry Terminal
topAttractions.description: The main departure point for ferries to the Gulf of Thailand islands. Donsak Pier is 70 km east of Surat Thani city and serves as the hub for Raja Ferry and Seatran Discovery car ferries to Koh Samui (2 hours) and Koh Phangan (3.5 hours). Lomprayah high-speed catamaran also operates fast connections. Bus-ferry combination tickets from Talad Kaset in the city center are the most convenient option for backpackers and budget travelers.
topAttractions.location: Donsak, 70 km east of Surat Thani city
topAttractions.entranceFee: Ferry tickets: Koh Samui 160 THB (Raja Ferry, car ferry), Koh Phangan 220 THB
tips: Buy combined bus+ferry tickets from Talad Kaset in Surat Thani city — avoids transport hassle to Donsak Pier
Raja Ferry runs car ferries almost continuously from 5 AM to 11 PM — no need to book ahead for foot passengers
Lomprayah catamaran is faster (1 hour to Samui) but more expensive (350-450 THB)

topAttractions.name: Saturday Night Market
topAttractions.description: Surat Thani's largest weekly market, held every Saturday evening along Na Muang Road and the surrounding streets near the city center. Dozens of stalls sell clothes, cosmetics, toys, household goods, and an excellent variety of southern Thai street food. Unlike tourist-facing markets in resort towns, this is a genuine local market where prices are set for Thais — expect to pay 30-80 THB for food, 100-300 THB for clothing.
topAttractions.location: Na Muang Road, Surat Thani city center
tips: Arrive between 5-7 PM when food stalls are freshest and before crowds peak
Try khanom jin (fermented rice noodles with southern-style curry) — a local specialty rarely found this well-made elsewhere
The market runs roughly 4-10 PM every Saturday

topAttractions.name: Tapi River Waterfront (Tapee Pier Area)
topAttractions.description: The Tapi River runs through the heart of Surat Thani, emptying into the Gulf of Thailand at Ban Don Bay. The riverfront near Tapee Pier comes alive in the evening with food stalls, fresh seafood restaurants, and a laidback local atmosphere. This is where Surat Thani locals eat fresh oysters, grilled river fish, and drink cold Singha overlooking the water. Longtail boat trips along the river are available from the pier.
topAttractions.location: Tapee Pier, Surat Thani city center
tips: Fresh oysters from the estuary farms are excellent and cheap — 50-80 THB for a plate
Evening is the best time — stalls set up from 5 PM and the river breeze makes the heat bearable
Longtail boat tours of the river mangroves run approximately 200-400 THB per person from the pier

topAttractions.name: Ban Don Floating Market
topAttractions.description: A traditional weekend canal market at Bang Bai Mai, 15 minutes outside the city center near Wat Bang Mai Bai temple. Open Sundays only from 9 AM to 3 PM, the market operates from traditional wooden boats on the canal selling local produce, snacks, and street food. Far less commercialized than famous floating markets near Bangkok — this is a genuine local experience where vendors are actual farmers and food producers from the surrounding area.
topAttractions.location: Bang Bai Mai canal, 15 km from Surat Thani city
tips: Go before 11 AM for the best atmosphere — later in the day the boats thin out
Bring small denomination baht — vendors prefer exact change
Combine with a visit to Wat Bang Mai Bai temple which is beside the market

travelTips: Talad Kaset (Markets 1 and 2) near the city center is the main hub for bus-ferry combo tickets to the islands — buy here rather than at the pier to save hassle
Night Boat service from Tapee Pier to Koh Samui (overnight departure around 11 PM) is an old-school budget option — check current operators as schedules change seasonally
Surat Thani's train station (Phun Phin) is 14 km west of the city — a red bus connects them every 15 minutes for 15 THB
The city has decent Grab and Bolt coverage for getting between the bus/train station and Talad Kaset
Durian season (May-July) turns Surat Thani into a durian mecca — the surrounding province produces some of Thailand's best Monthong and Chanee varieties
If transiting to Khao Sok, most guesthouses in Surat Thani will store your luggage for free while you do a day trip
The Chak Phra Festival in October is one of the most spectacular events in southern Thailand — book accommodation weeks in advance if visiting during this period
ATM machines are plentiful in the city center (220 THB fee per withdrawal) — stock up before heading to Donsak Pier where options are limited
gettingThere: Surat Thani Airport (URT) has multiple daily flights from Bangkok — Thai AirAsia, Nok Air, and Bangkok Airways from 1,200 THB one-way (1 hour 20 minutes). Airport to city center is 30 minutes by shared minivan (100 THB) or private taxi (400-500 THB). Bangkok to Surat Thani by overnight train takes 9-12 hours (from 400 THB sleeper class) and arrives at Phun Phin station 14 km from the city. Buses from Bangkok's Southern Bus Terminal take 10-12 hours (from 500 THB). Surat Thani is also a major stop on the southern bus route connecting Bangkok with Phuket, Krabi, and Hat Yai.
gettingAround: Within Surat Thani city, songthaews (shared pickup trucks) cover the main routes for 15-25 THB. Motorcycle taxis cluster at major intersections and handle short trips for 30-60 THB. Grab works reasonably well in the city center. For Donsak Pier (70 km), combined bus-ferry tickets from Talad Kaset are the easiest option and include transport to the pier. To reach Phun Phin train station (14 km), take the red bus from the city center for 15 THB. Car and scooter rental is available near Talad Kaset for around 150-300 THB/day — useful for visiting Wat Phra Borommathat Chaiya (54 km north) and Khao Sok (80 km northwest).
